frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: covariance structure of neural populations shapes encoded stimulus information

frameworks.name: noisy nonlinear circuit propagation
frameworks.one_line: information transmission across spike-generating nonlinearity and downstream noise

frameworks.name: differential correlations
frameworks.one_line: correlations known to reduce encoded information yet can aid propagation

frameworks.name: redundancy vs synergy in codes
frameworks.one_line: redundant and synergistic codes evaluated for robustness against noise corruption

core_question: What upstream covariance structures optimize information propagation through noisy nonlinear downstream circuits?
key_assumptions: downstream stage adds spike-generating nonlinearity and noise corruption
informativeness and propagation are distinct features of population codes
covariance structure of upstream cells governs both encoding and propagation
manipulations: covariance structures of upstream population responses
presence vs absence of differential correlations
redundant vs synergistic population code configurations
key_findings: identified a family of covariances that optimize information propagation
this optimal family includes differential correlations that reduce encoded information
redundant codes can be fragile and synergistic codes can optimize robustness
interpretations: codes maximizing encoded information differ from codes maximizing propagation
redundancy is neither necessary nor sufficient for robust population codes
